short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $49.84B in FY2026 Q1, +10.0% YoY — accelerating trajectory entering the next print.
Next-quarter consensus: $51.42B (+3.2% vs current Q) — set by 25 broker contributors.
EPS consensus next qtr: $6.24 vs current $5.94.
Margin context: 33.1% net margin — expansion likely.
Next earnings: 2026-08-12 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is constructive — consensus is achievable based on current run-rate.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $187B — in cyclical drawdown.
Sell-side: FY1 $206B → FY2 $220B (+7.0% multi-year top-line implied).
Forward EBIT margin: 28.0% — structural premium business over the long-cycle view.
7-quarter run-rate CAGR: -0.5% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Financials): Rate-cycle position + capital-return runway define the 1-3 year setup.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if growth durability + margin expansion both hold.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · trading window)

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 35.7% — normal.
Put/Call OI: 0.63 — call-heavy positioning (consensus bullish).
30-day implied move: ±6.0% ($281.95 – $318.11) — the 1-σ range the options market is pricing.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ DTE · LEAPS / structural)
bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 27.2% across 10 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 10 dates spanning 98–616 DTE.
90-day implied move: ±13.7% ($258.85 – $341.21).
398-day implied move: ±28.5% ($214.56 – $385.50) — the long-dated 1-σ range.
Sell-side PT vs options-implied range: consensus $240 (-20.0% upside). Compare to the 90+ DTE implied range above.
Term structure in backwardation (-8.5pp front→back) — near-term event premium; long-dated vol cheaper.
long_term.strategy: Bear put spread 6-month: long $285 / short $255. Defined-cost downside positioning; cheaper than outright long puts; captures 10% further downside efficiently.
